channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Nanded Helicopter Plant: Rs 4,250 Cr Project Partner Has Tiny Capital, New Setup
item.description: A Rs 4,250 crore helicopter manufacturing plant is planned for Nanded, Maharashtra, aiming to create 2,000 jobs and support national manufacturing goals. However, the project faces questions due to its partner, Retavia Private Limited, a company incorporated in September 2025 with only INR 1 lakh paid-up capital. This partnership raises serious doubts about the project's execution and funding.

item.title: IOB Cuts 1-Month Lending Rate by 10 Bps to 8.20% from March 15
item.description: Indian Overseas Bank (IOB) has cut its one-month Marginal Cost of Funds based Lending Rate (MCLR) by 10 basis points to 8.20%, effective March 15, 2026. All other lending rates remain unchanged. The move aims to reduce short-term borrowing costs for customers.

item.title: India's Sunflower Oil Imports Fall 51% as Prices Surge, Supplies Hit
item.description: India's sunflower oil imports dropped 51% in February 2026 to 1.45 lakh tonnes. Higher global prices (up 17%) and a weaker rupee increased import costs. Conflicts in the Middle East and Black Sea worsened shipping disruptions and freight costs. With Russia and Ukraine supplying most of its needs, India is now looking for alternative sources like Mercosur nations.

item.title: Scan Projects Seeks NCLT Merger Approval for Chandrapur Industry
item.description: Scan Projects Limited has filed an application with the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) Chandigarh for its proposed merger with Chandrapur Industry Private Limited. The merger plan, approved by both companies' boards on September 18, 2025, aims to combine operations, with Scan Projects absorbing Chandrapur Industry. This filing follows a prior withdrawal, showing a renewed push for the company's restructuring.

item.title: NSE Hires Record 20 Banks for Mega IPO After Regulatory Approval
item.description: India's National Stock Exchange (NSE) has hired a record 20 merchant banks for its long-awaited IPO. This follows regulatory approval in January 2026 and years of delays. The large team aims to manage the complexities of a broad shareholder base and past regulatory issues for an estimated ₹23,000 crore Offer for Sale.

item.title: Ai+ Smartphone Taps Optiemus for India Manufacturing Scale
item.description: Homegrown Ai+ Smartphone is partnering with Optiemus Electronics to manufacture its devices, including smartphones, tablets, and wearables, in India. The ₹125 crore, five-year agreement targets three million units annually from April 2026, creating over 1,200 jobs and bolstering the 'Make in India' initiative. This strategic move supports Ai+'s ambition to scale its product ecosystem and challenge established global players from its Noida-based manufacturing hub.

item.title: Cholamandalam Financial Holdings Debt Rating Confirmed IND AA+/Stable
item.description: India Ratings & Research has reaffirmed Cholamandalam Financial Holdings Limited's (CFHL) credit rating for its Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs) at 'IND AA+/Stable'. This affirmation covers ₹2,000 million in NCDs, signaling continued financial strength and a stable outlook for the company's debt obligations, reflecting its solid position within the Murugappa Group.

item.title: Harish Textile EGM OKs Share Issue; Promoter Votes Invalid
item.description: Harish Textile Engineers Limited shareholders have overwhelmingly approved a preferential equity share issuance at an Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) held on March 11, 2026. The resolution passed with 99.97% of valid votes in favour. However, a significant number of votes from the Promoter and Promoter Group were declared invalid, raising governance-related questions.

item.title: Shiva Granito Export: Director Satish Heda Resigns Amid Board Changes
item.description: Shiva Granito Export Limited announced the resignation of Independent Director Mr. Satish Heda, effective March 10, 2026, citing personal reasons. This departure follows a series of recent board changes, including other independent directors resigning due to similar circumstances. The company, a granite and marble exporter, faces ongoing scrutiny regarding its governance compliance and past operational challenges.

item.title: Zee Learn Subsidiary Loses Major Clients, Operations and Revenue Suffer
item.description: Zee Learn's subsidiary, Liberium Global Resources, expects a significant hit to its operations and revenue as key clients signal they won't continue services. The company is evaluating mitigation strategies, a move that raises concerns given the subsidiary's role in HR outsourcing and talent management.

item.title: Unifinz Capital Grants 40.5 Lakh ESOPs to Employees
item.description: Unifinz Capital India Limited approved granting 40.5 lakh employee stock options (ESOPs) on March 12, 2026. Each option has a ₹10 exercise price and can be used within three years of vesting. The company hopes these options will boost employee motivation and link their rewards to its success.

item.title: Ghushine Fintrrade Shareholders Back Capital Boost and Add Independent Director
item.description: Shareholders at Ghushine Fintrrade Ocean Limited have given unanimous approval for key company changes through a postal ballot. The approved resolutions include raising the authorized share capital and updating its Memorandum and Articles of Association (MoA/AoA), along with appointing Mr. Sagar Kumbhani as a Non-Executive Independent Director.

item.title: Private Credit's Structure Shields It From Bank-Run Fears, But Risks Grow
item.description: Concerns about private credit's systemic risk are eased by its illiquid nature, which slows down investor outflows. Goldman Sachs notes that about 80% of direct lending capital is in non-redeemable funds, providing a buffer. However, "true" default rates are climbing, regulators are paying closer attention, and this market, untested through a full credit cycle, faces growing challenges.

item.title: Quadrant Future Tek KAVACH System in Final Indian Railways Trials; Order Book ₹898 Cr
item.description: Quadrant Future Tek Limited is in the final phase of passenger field trials for its KAVACH safety system with Indian Railways. Success will bring formal certification from RDSO, allowing wider commercial use. The company reported an order book of ₹897.88 crore as of February 28, 2026, including major orders for KAVACH onboard equipment.

item.title: Krishival Foods Approves ₹45 Cr Loan to Subsidiary, Seeks Member Approval
item.description: Krishival Foods Limited's board approved a ₹45 crore working capital loan for its subsidiary, Melt 'N' Mellow Foods. With a 10% interest rate, the loan is set to convert into equity shares, pending member approval. The company also cleared related party deals for a factory purchase and sales, boosting its ice cream division.

item.title: Arvind Ltd. Shareholders Approve ESOPs, Subsidiary Asset Sales with Over 99% Vote
item.description: Arvind Limited shareholders overwhelmingly approved four special resolutions by postal ballot. The votes greenlit changes to the company's ESOP scheme and allowed subsidiary Arvind Advanced Materials Limited to sell or lease assets. All proposals received over 99% of votes cast, though a significant number of invalid votes were noted.

item.title: Balaji Amines Halts Production Amid West Asia Conflict Supply Issues
item.description: Balaji Amines Ltd. suspended operations at select manufacturing facilities on March 12, 2026, due to critical disruptions in ammonia supply. The core issue stems from global logistics and supply chain failures exacerbated by the ongoing West Asia conflict. While the company's stock closed marginally higher, management is assessing an unquantifiable financial and operational impact and exploring alternative procurement routes for the vital raw material. This event highlights significant supply chain vulnerabilities for the specialty chemicals producer.

item.title: BlackRock's New ETH ETF Offers Staking Rewards to Investors
item.description: BlackRock's iShares Staked Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHB) commenced trading on Nasdaq on March 12, 2026. This new ETF integrates Ethereum staking rewards directly into its structure, aiming to attract yield-seeking investors. Unlike previous spot Ether ETFs, ETHB allows for potential passive income generation. It enters a competitive market with a 0.25% sponsor fee, temporarily reduced to 0.12% for the first year, and a 10% fee on staking rewards. BlackRock's entry with a staking product could accelerate institutional adoption of yield-generating digital assets.

item.title: Manorama Industries Plans ₹500 Cr Fundraise Following Strong Q4 Results
item.description: Manorama Industries announced plans to raise up to ₹500 crore by issuing securities. This follows robust Q4 2025 financial results, which showed a 73.3% revenue increase and a 131.1% profit jump. Despite the strong performance, the company's shares dipped slightly on the announcement day. Investors are weighing its high valuation multiples and mixed analyst sentiment against the backdrop of geopolitical volatility in the specialty chemicals sector.

item.title: JK Cement Schedules Investor Meetings in Singapore for March 2026
item.description: JK Cement Limited is holding investor and analyst meetings in Singapore from March 23 to March 25, 2026. The company's management will use this non-deal roadshow to share insights into its strategic direction and operational performance.

item.title: Addi Industries Directors Win Strong Shareholder Approval in Record Vote
item.description: Addi Industries shareholders have decisively approved the appointment of four directors through a postal ballot. The vote confirmed Sandeep Mittal as Whole Time Director, Rajat Goyal as Director, and Jai Kishan and Preeti Jain as Non-Executive Independent Directors for five-year terms. The robust turnout signals strong shareholder confidence in the company's direction.

item.title: India's Inflation Rises to 3.21% Driven by Food and Services
item.description: India's retail inflation rose to 3.21% in February, a jump from 2.75% in January. This increase was mainly due to higher prices for food, beverages, personal care items, and services. Despite some items seeing lower prices, the overall trend shows rising costs impacting households.

item.title: Mahindra EPC Irrigation Lands ₹2.22 Crore Community Micro-Irrigation Deal
item.description: Mahindra EPC Irrigation Ltd. has secured a contract worth approximately ₹2.22 crore (excluding taxes) for a community micro-irrigation project. The project is expected to be completed within six to twelve months after site handover, reinforcing the company's efforts in the micro-irrigation market.

item.title: Quality Power Asks Shareholders to Re-appoint Director Rajendra Iyer
item.description: Quality Power Electrical Equipments Limited is seeking shareholder approval to re-appoint Rajendra Sheshadri Iyer as an Independent Director. His second term would be for five years, starting February 15, 2026. Shareholders can vote electronically between March 14 and April 12, 2026, with results due by April 15, 2026. This re-appointment is key for continuity in experienced leadership.

item.title: Gold's 20-Year Surge: High Returns Mask Real Costs and Risks
item.description: Gold has delivered a remarkable 16.35% annual return over the last two decades, turning ₹1 lakh into ₹20.7 lakh. However, this stellar performance is shadowed by compounding costs like making charges and GST for physical gold, and broader market dynamics affecting other asset classes. Equities, though volatile, offer a different growth trajectory, while fixed deposits provide stability but lag inflation. Investors face a nuanced choice beyond simple historical returns.

item.title: Orient Bell Denies Hidden News After Logistics Vendor Clarification
item.description: Orient Bell Limited (OBL) has issued a clarification to stock exchanges, addressing a news item concerning its digital logistics transformation. The company confirmed the onboarding of vendor 'Traqo' in December 2024, with the arrangement set to continue until March 31, 2026. OBL emphasized this was a standard business activity conducted on an arm's length basis, denying any specific negotiations or material developments requiring disclosure. The company stated that no undisclosed information could explain stock price movements and that the news article has no material impact on its operations or financial position.

item.title: Apple's $599 MacBook Neo Aims for Students and Ecosystem Growth
item.description: Apple's $599 MacBook Neo represents a new strategy to make its computers more accessible, using established A18 chip technology. The goal is to bring more users into Apple's ecosystem, particularly in higher education, rather than directly compete with cheaper Chromebooks in K-12. Analysts expect this to increase Mac shipments.

item.title: Gandhar Oil: 9-Month Profit Tops Full Year; Risks Mount from Costs
item.description: Gandhar Oil Refinery has reported its latest financial update, highlighting that its Profit After Tax (PAT) for the nine months ended December 31, 2025, at ₹92.74 crore, has already surpassed the full fiscal year 2025 PAT of ₹79.31 crore. However, the company also flagged significant geopolitical risks, including a sharp ~20% increase in base oil prices over 15 days, forex volatility, and rising freight charges, which could impact future profitability.

item.title: Gujarat Apollo Targets ₹300 Crore Revenue Surge With Dual-Sector Growth
item.description: Gujarat Apollo Industries is launching a major growth initiative using a 'Dual-Engine' strategy in infrastructure and agriculture equipment. The company expects revenue to increase by over ₹300 Crores from product development and facility upgrades, supported by a ₹26 Crore capital investment. This strategy diversifies its business and aims to capture more market share in key sectors.

item.title: India Cuts Strait of Hormuz Reliance, Boosts Energy Security
item.description: New Delhi has significantly bolstered its energy security, reducing reliance on the Strait of Hormuz to 30% of crude imports and sourcing from 40 nations. Domestic LPG production has surged 25% to meet demand, supported by strategic reserves of 250 million barrels. Despite geopolitical volatility and crude price surges, major oil PSUs like IOCL, BPCL, and HPCL maintain robust operations and attract analyst 'Buy' ratings, trading at deep value P/E multiples compared to sector peers.

item.title: Arvind Fashions Shareholders Approve ESOPs, Stock Purchase Plans
item.description: Arvind Fashions Limited shareholders overwhelmingly approved seven special resolutions. These include plans for Employee Stock Option Schemes (ESOPs) in 2016, 2022, and 2025, plus secondary share acquisitions through a trust. The strong vote allows the company to advance key talent management and retention efforts.

item.title: LA TIM METAL Promoter Buys 6.3 Lakh Shares, Increases Stake to 0.51%
item.description: LA TIM METAL & INDUSTRIES LIMITED's promoter, La-tim Lifestyle & Resorts Limited, bought 630,600 shares on March 12, 2026. This raises the promoter's total holding to 669,600 shares, or 0.51% of the company. The purchase signals promoter confidence, despite the company's recent financial losses and weak growth.

item.title: EPACK Durable Compliance Officer Jyoti Verma Resigns
item.description: EPACK Durable Limited's Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, Jyoti Verma, has resigned to pursue career growth. Her last day is March 23, 2026. This departure marks a key leadership change for the consumer durables maker, underscoring the importance of strong compliance oversight.

item.title: Bhageria Industries SEBI Case Hearing Pushed to May 2026
item.description: Bhageria Industries Ltd's hearing for its case involving a SEBI order has been adjourned by the Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) to May 5-7, 2026. This extends the stay on the SEBI adjudication order from March 31, 2023, delaying the final resolution of the legal proceedings.

item.title: Tata Power's Growth Surge Faces Valuation and Debt Hurdles
item.description: Tata Power reported strong gains in its rooftop solar business and improved profitability from its Odisha power distribution operations, alongside progress on its 5.5 GW project pipeline. However, the company operates with significant debt (1.6x Debt/Equity) and a higher valuation P/E ratio (32-34x) compared to peers like NTPC (15x) and Power Grid (17x). While Tata Power plans to reduce debt below 1.0x by FY27, its current leverage and premium valuation present potential execution and market risks.

item.title: Kotyark Industries Ltd. Joins NSE/BSE Main Boards, Unlocking Wider Investor Access
item.description: Kotyark Industries Limited is set to move from the NSE Emerge platform to the main boards of both NSE and BSE on March 12, 2026. This strategic migration aims to increase the company's market visibility and improve the liquidity of its shares, opening doors for broader institutional and retail investor participation.

item.title: Zee Media Names Prasanna Raghav Head of New Business Initiatives
item.description: Zee Media Corporation Limited has appointed Prasanna Raghav as its new Business Head – New Initiatives, effective March 13, 2026. Raghav, a media veteran with over 20 years of experience in content innovation and strategic leadership, returns to Zee Media to spearhead exploration of new business verticals and drive growth.

item.title: Max Financial Services Board Greenlights ₹2,000 Crore Boost for Axis Max Life
item.description: Max Financial Services' board has approved a fundraising of up to ₹2,000 crore, primarily to strengthen its subsidiary, Axis Max Life Insurance. The capital aims to fuel the insurer's growth and expansion. This move also involves increasing the company's authorised share capital, which is needed for issuing new shares.

item.title: Devyani International to Meet Investors at CLSA India Consumer Tour March 18
item.description: Devyani International will join CLSA India's Consumer Tour on March 18, 2026, in Gurugram. The company stated it will not share any sensitive financial or business information during the event, emphasizing its outreach to the investment community.

item.title: Metropolis Healthcare Plans Investor Meeting March 23, 2026
item.description: Metropolis Healthcare Limited will hold an Analyst and Institutional Investor Meeting on March 23, 2026. The discussion will focus on publicly available information to ensure transparency and keep stakeholders updated.
